summary: All Australian States & Offence Categories Testing Complete. Backend API verified: GET /api/states returns all 8 states, GET /api/offence-categories returns all 11 categories, GET /api/offence-framework/{category}?state={state} returns correct state-specific legislation (Victoria=Crimes Act 1958 (Vic), Queensland=Criminal Code Act 1899 (Qld)). Frontend landing page displays all state badges (NSW, VIC, QLD, SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT, Federal) and offence categories.
passed_tests: GET /api/states returns all 8 Australian states/territories (NSw, VIC, QLD, SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT)
GET /api/states returns correct abbreviations for all states
GET /api/offence-categories returns all 11 offence categories
GET /api/offence-categories returns correct structure (id, name, description, offences)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=vic returns Crimes Act 1958 (Vic) NOT NSW legislation
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=qld returns Criminal Code Act 1899 (Qld)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=nsw returns Crimes Act 1900 (NSW)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=sa returns Criminal Law Consolidation Act 1935 (SA)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=wa returns Criminal Code Act Compilation Act 1913 (WA)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=tas returns Criminal Code Act 1924 (Tas)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=nt returns Criminal Code Act 1983 (NT)
GET /api/offence-framework/homicide?state=act returns Crimes Act 1900 (ACT)
Appeal framework returns Victoria-specific info (Criminal Procedure Act 2009)
Appeal framework returns Queensland-specific info (Criminal Code Act 1899 Part 10)
Common appeal grounds included in all responses
Drug offences return VIC-specific Drugs Poisons and Controlled Substances Act 1981
Drug offences return QLD-specific Drugs Misuse Act 1986
Fraud returns VIC-specific Crimes Act 1958
Domestic violence returns VIC-specific Family Violence Protection Act 2008
Terrorism offences return Commonwealth Criminal Code Act 1995
Invalid category returns 404
Homicide endpoint works for all 8 states
Common categories (homicide, assault, drug, fraud, DV, driving) work for Victoria
Landing page loads correctly
Landing page shows all 8 state badges (NSW, VIC, QLD, SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT)
Landing page shows Federal badge
Landing page shows all offence category badges (Homicide, Assault, Sexual Offences, etc.)
Dashboard state selector available in New Case dialog (data-testid=new-case-state)
LegalFrameworkViewer component has state selector (data-testid=state-selector)

states_api.details: GET /api/states returns all 8 Australian states/territories with correct names and abbreviations

offence_categories_api.details: GET /api/offence-categories returns all 11 categories: homicide, assault, sexual_offences, robbery_theft, drug_offences, fraud_dishonesty, firearms_weapons, domestic_violence, public_order, terrorism, driving_offences

state_specific_legislation.details: GET /api/offence-framework/{category}?state={state} correctly returns state-specific legislation. KEY TESTS PASSED: VIC returns 'Crimes Act 1958 (Vic)', QLD returns 'Criminal Code Act 1899 (Qld)'

dashboard_state_selector.details: New Case dialog has state selector dropdown (data-testid='new-case-state') that fetches from /api/states

legal_framework_viewer_state_selector.details: LegalFrameworkViewer component has state selector (data-testid='state-selector') allowing switching between states

case_detail_state_badge.details: Case detail page displays state badge alongside offence badges using blue styling (bg-blue-50 text-blue-700 border-blue-200)

critical_code_review_comments: State-specific legislation properly implemented in offence_framework.py with all 8 states
API endpoint correctly extracts {state}_legislation key for dynamic state legislation lookup
Dashboard fetches states via fetchStates() on component mount
CaseDetail displays state badge with blue styling for visual distinction from offence badges
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_state_offence_framework.py - Created comprehensive test file with 35 tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Australian states (NSW, VIC, QLD, SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT) and all 11 offence categories fully tested and working. State-specific legislation returns correct laws for each jurisdiction. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_state_offence_framework.py available for regression testing with 35 comprehensive tests.
